root_text.sn35.134:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati devadahaṁ nāma sakyānaṁ nigamo.
root_text.sn35.134:1.3: “nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sabbesaṁyeva bhikkhūnaṁ chasu phassāyatanesu appamādena karaṇīyanti vadāmi, na ca panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sabbesaṁyeva bhikkhūnaṁ chasu phassāyatanesu nāppamādena karaṇīyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.134:1.4: Ye te, bhikkhave, bhikkhū arahanto khīṇāsavā vusitavanto katakaraṇīyā ohitabhārā anuppattasadatthā parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojanā sammadaññāvimuttā, tesāhaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ chasu phassāyatanesu nāppamādena karaṇīyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.134:1.6: Kataṁ tesaṁ appamādena, abhabbā te pamajjituṁ.
root_text.sn35.134:1.7: Ye ca kho te, bhikkhave, bhikkhū sekkhā appattamānasā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ patthayamānā viharanti, tesāhaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ chasu phassāyatanesu appamādena karaṇīyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.134:1.9: Santi, bhikkhave, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā manoramāpi, amanoramāpi.
root_text.sn35.134:1.10: Tyāssa phussa phussa cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhanti.
root_text.sn35.134:1.11: Cetaso apariyādānā āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
root_text.sn35.134:1.12: Imaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appamādaphalaṁ sampassamāno tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ chasu phassāyatanesu appamādena karaṇīyanti vadāmi …pe…
root_text.sn35.134:1.13: santi, bhikkhave, manoviññeyyā dhammā manoramāpi amanoramāpi.
root_text.sn35.134:1.14: Tyāssa phussa phussa cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhanti.
root_text.sn35.134:1.15: Cetaso apariyādānā āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
root_text.sn35.134:1.16: Imaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appamādaphalaṁ sampassamāno tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ chasu phassāyatanesu appamādena karaṇīyanti vadāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.134:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near the Sakyan town named Devadaha.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn35.134:1.3: “When it comes to the six fields of contact, mendicants, I don’t say that all mendicants have work to do with diligence, nor do I say that none of them have work to do with diligence.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.4: I say that, when it comes to the six fields of contact, mendicants don’t have work to do with diligence if they are perfected, with defilements ended, having completed the spiritual journey, done what had to be done, laid down the burden, achieved their heart’s goal, utterly ended the fetter of continued existence, and become rightly freed through enlightenment.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.6: They’ve done their work with diligence, and are incapable of negligence.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.7: I say that, when it comes to the six fields of contact, mendicants do have work to do with diligence if they are trainees, who haven’t achieved their heart’s desire, but live aspiring to the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.9: There are sights known by the eye that are pleasant and also those that are unpleasant.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.10: Though experiencing them again and again they don’t occupy the mind.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.11: Their energy is roused up and unflagging, their mindfulness is established and lucid, their body is tranquil and undisturbed, and their mind is immersed in samādhi and unified.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.12: Seeing this fruit of diligence, I say that those mendicants have work to do with diligence when it comes to the six fields of contact. …
translation_text.sn35.134:1.13: There are ideas known by the mind that are pleasant and also those that are unpleasant.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.14: Though experiencing them again and again they don’t occupy the mind.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.15: Their energy is roused up and unflagging, their mindfulness is established and lucid, their body is tranquil and undisturbed, and their mind is immersed in samādhi and unified.
translation_text.sn35.134:1.16: Seeing this fruit of diligence, I say that those mendicants have work to do with diligence when it comes to the six fields of contact.”

variant_text.sn35.134:1.7: sekkhā → sekhā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr)
